# ReadingBat Template

> A Kotlin/Ktor template for teachers to author ReadingBat.com content — a code-reading practice site where students trace code execution rather than write code. Supports Python, Java, and Kotlin challenges.

## Overview

ReadingBat Template provides a starting point for educators to create their own ReadingBat content. Challenge content is defined using a Kotlin DSL in a single file, and challenge source code lives in language-specific directories. The readingbat-core library handles parsing, answer generation, and web serving.

## Quick Start

- Build: ./gradlew build -xtest
- Run server: ./gradlew run
- Run tests: ./gradlew --rerun-tasks check
- Build fat JAR: ./gradlew uberjar

## Key Files

- src/main/kotlin/Content.kt: Central content definition using the readingBatContent DSL
- src/main/kotlin/ContentServer.kt: Server entry point (delegates to ReadingBatServer)
- src/test/kotlin/ContentTests.kt: Kotest-based tests that verify all challenges
- build.gradle.kts: Gradle build script (group/version live in gradle.properties)
- gradle.properties: Project group and version
- settings.gradle.kts: Repository configuration with FAIL_ON_PROJECT_REPOS enforcement
- gradle/libs.versions.toml: Dependency version catalog with [bundles] testing group
- CHANGELOG.md / RELEASE_NOTES.md: Release history and per-version notes
- python/: Python challenge source files
- src/main/java/: Java challenge source files
- src/main/kotlin/kgroup1/: Kotlin challenge source files

## Content DSL Structure

readingBatContent {
  python/java/kotlin {
    group("Name") {
      packageName = "directory_name"
      description = "Markdown description"
      challenge("FileName") {
        returnType = StringType|BooleanType|IntType  // required for Python/Kotlin, inferred for Java
        codingBatEquiv = "pXXXXXX"                   // optional CodingBat cross-reference
      }
      includeFiles = "glob*.ext"                     // bulk import (Java, types inferred)
      includeFilesWithType = "glob*.ext" returns Type // bulk import with explicit return type
    }
  }
}

## Architecture

The repo property in Content.kt switches between GitHubRepo (production, reads from GitHub) and FileSystemSource (local dev, reads from filesystem). Challenge packageName maps directly to filesystem directories. The readingbat-core and readingbat-kotest libraries (from Maven Central) provide the DSL, server, and test framework. Test dependencies are exposed as the libs.bundles.testing bundle in libs.versions.toml.

## Tech Stack

- Language: Kotlin (JVM 17)
- Web framework: Ktor
- Test framework: Kotest (StringSpec style, JUnit5 runner)
- Build: Gradle 9.5.0 with version catalog (libs.versions.toml) and properties-driven version
- Deployment: Heroku-compatible (system.properties, fat JAR via shadow plugin)
- Current version: 1.7.0
